verdict_reasoning: TGFB2 is a secreted ligand of the TGF-beta superfamily. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or direct membrane-anchoring mechanism. Although the latent TGF-beta complex (TGFB2 + LAP + LTBP) can associate with cell surfaces and extracellular matrix through LTBP interactions with integrins and ECM components, TGFB2 itself is recruited there only transiently via non-covalent binding to LTBP and indirect ECM sequestration, not via its own mechanism. The active form is soluble. No evidence of stable direct membrane anchoring or MHC presentation as a clinically engaged tumor antigen.
